items.q: Which type of nuclear radiation is most ionising?
items.correct: Alpha radiation — large, doubly charged particles cause the most ionisation per unit distance.
items.misconception: Gamma is most ionising because it can travel furthest and pass through almost anything.
items.random_wrong: Beta radiation is most ionising because it consists of fast electrons.
items.why: Students conflate penetrating power with ionising power — gamma penetrates most because it ionises least, the opposite of intuition.

items.q: If a car has negative acceleration, what does that necessarily mean?
items.correct: Its acceleration is in the negative direction (often opposite to the chosen positive direction); it may be slowing down or speeding up in reverse.
items.misconception: Negative acceleration always means the car is slowing down.
items.random_wrong: Negative acceleration means the car is moving backwards.
items.why: Students conflate 'deceleration' with 'negative acceleration'; sign indicates direction relative to the chosen axis, not necessarily slowing.

items.q: What is the difference between heat and temperature?
items.correct: Heat is the energy transferred due to temperature difference; temperature measures average kinetic energy of particles.
items.misconception: Heat and temperature are the same thing — both measure how hot something is.
items.random_wrong: Heat is measured in degrees Celsius and temperature in Joules.
items.why: Everyday language uses 'heat' and 'temperature' interchangeably ('turn up the heat'), so students don't distinguish them physically.

items.q: A car travels 4 km north then 3 km east. What is its displacement magnitude?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 5 km. “7 km” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which of the following is a scalar quantity?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Kinetic energy. “Acceleration” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
